inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Deletion of the mandated `copyDir` helper — The PR removes the `export function copyDir(srcDir, destDir)` entirely. This is the exact helper that `.review-rules/copy.md` requires all recursive directory copies to go through. Removing it strips the sanctioned helper and forces every caller onto `fs.cpSync`. The rule explicitly states that 'a change that removes `copyDir` … must be flagged.'

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Behavioral regression in publicDir copy: symlink semantics flip and lost self-recursion guard — The call `copyDir(publicDir, outDir)` is replaced with `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })`. This introduces two regressions vs the removed helper: (1) Symlink semantics flip — the old `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` (dereferences symlinks) and `fs.copyFileSync`, so a symlinked entry was copied as the target's content. `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, so it copies symlinks as symlinks, producing dangling symlinks in `outDir` instead of file contents. The rule explicitly calls out symlink-following as behavior consumers depend on. (2) Lost self-recursion guard — the old helper had `if (srcFile === destDir) continue` to avoid copying a directory into itself when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir`. `fs.cpSync` has no equivalent and will error or recurse infinitely.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Same `copyDir` swap for template scaffolding with symlink regression — The call `copy(...)` is replaced with `fs.cpSync(..., { recursive: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })`, and the local `copy` and `copyDir` helpers are deleted. Template scaffolding is one of the two call sites named in `.review-rules/copy.md`. The same symlink-dereference regression applies — the old local `copy` used `statSync.isDirectory()` and `copyFileSync`, which followed symlinks; `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, changing symlink behavior. The rule requires this swap be flagged, and the same Windows non-ASCII-path concerns that motivated the rule apply here.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

All three issues stem from replacing the mandated `copyDir` helper with `fs.cpSync`, violating `.review-rules/copy.md` and introducing two behavioral regressions: symlink semantics flip (dereference: true → false) and loss of the self-recursion guard in the Vite outDir copy path. The reviewer recommends rejecting the PR unless the rule is updated first and the old observable behavior is matched.

**3** finding(s): 🔴 3  ·  🟡 0  ·  🔵 0

- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:624` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Deletion of the mandated `copyDir` helper — The PR removes the `export function copyDir(srcDir, destDir)` entirely. This is the exact helper that `.review-rules/copy.md` requires all recursive directory copies to go through. Removing it strips the sanctioned helper and forces every caller onto `fs.cpSync`. The rule explicitly states that 'a change that removes `copyDir` … must be flagged.'
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:86` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Behavioral regression in publicDir copy: symlink semantics flip and lost self-recursion guard — The call `copyDir(publicDir, outDir)` is replaced with `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })`. This introduces two regressions vs the removed helper: (1) Symlink semantics flip — the old `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` (dereferences symlinks) and `fs.copyFileSync`, so a symlinked entry was copied as the target's content. `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, so it copies symlinks as symlinks, producing dangling symlinks in `outDir` instead of file contents. The rule explicitly calls out symlink-following as behavior consumers depend on. (2) Lost self-recursion guard — the old helper had `if (srcFile === destDir) continue` to avoid copying a directory into itself when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir`. `fs.cpSync` has no equivalent and will error or recurse infinitely.
- 🔴 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:644` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Same `copyDir` swap for template scaffolding with symlink regression — The call `copy(...)` is replaced with `fs.cpSync(..., { recursive: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })`, and the local `copy` and `copyDir` helpers are deleted. Template scaffolding is one of the two call sites named in `.review-rules/copy.md`. The same symlink-dereference regression applies — the old local `copy` used `statSync.isDirectory()` and `copyFileSync`, which followed symlinks; `fs.cpSync` defaults to `dereference: false`, changing symlink behavior. The rule requires this swap be flagged, and the same Windows non-ASCII-path concerns that motivated the rule apply here.
